Class VictoryScene

java.lang.Object
gui.VictoryScene
public class VictoryScene extends Object
The VictoryScene class displays the victory screen after the player wins a battle.

This scene serves as a transition screen that shows the current hero team and then allows the player to proceed to the upgrade screen.

Main responsibilities:

  • Set the game state to GameState.VICTORY
  • Clear any previously selected upgrade hero via GameEngine.setUpgradeHero(Heroes)
  • Render a victory background and a title
  • Display the current hero team icons from GameEngine.getHeroTEAM()
  • Provide a "Next" button to navigate to UpgradeScene.show(Stage, GameEngine)

UI notes:

  • This scene uses a fixed window size of 1280 by 720 and disables resizing
  • Hero icons are displayed with a drop shadow for depth

    • VictoryScene

      public VictoryScene()

  • Method Details

    • show

      public static void show(javafx.stage.Stage stage, GameEngine gameEngine)
      Shows the victory screen.

      This method:

      • Updates the global game state to GameState.VICTORY
      • Resets the selected upgrade hero to null
      • Builds and displays the JavaFX UI

      Navigation:

      • Clicking "Next" opens UpgradeScene
      Parameters:
      stage - the primary JavaFX stage used to show the scene
      gameEngine - the main game engine instance used for navigation and global state

    • createCharacterImage

      public static javafx.scene.image.ImageView createCharacterImage(String imagePath1)
      Creates a hero icon ImageView for display on the victory screen.

      The icon is styled with a drop shadow and fixed sizing.

      Parameters:
      imagePath1 - resource path to the hero icon image
      Returns:
      an ImageView configured for this scene

    • createButton

      private static javafx.scene.control.Button createButton(String path)
      Creates an image-based button with hover scaling, glow effect, and press animation.

      This helper is used for the "Next" button.

      Parameters:
      path - resource path of the button image
      Returns:
      a styled JavaFX Button
